title: Physio-Control LIFEPAK 12 - Lead/ECG Cable Connection Errors
description: Guide for troubleshooting intermittent or faulty ECG lead connections on the LIFEPAK 12 defibrillator using external checks before assuming internal faults.

steps.title: Verify Lead Connection Orientation
steps.instructions: Ensure all ECG lead connectors are fully seated in their ports. Check that patient electrodes are attached in the correct positions. Why: Loose or misaligned connectors are the most common cause of lead errors.

steps.title: Inspect ECG Leads and Cables for Damage
steps.instructions: Look for cuts, kinks, exposed wires, or broken snaps/pins. Gently flex the cable while observing if the error appears. Why: Damaged cables can cause intermittent or false lead errors.

steps.title: Swap Leads or Cables
steps.instructions: Use a known-good ECG cable or set of leads. If the error clears, the original cable is likely defective.

steps.title: Check Connector Pins and Ports
steps.instructions: Inspect lead pins for corrosion, bent pins, or debris. Clean gently with isopropyl alcohol if dirty. Why: Corrosion or debris can block proper signal transmission.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn the LIFEPAK 12 off, wait 10–15 seconds, then power on. Observe if the lead error persists. Why: Some intermittent errors clear after a reset.

steps.title: Verify Patient Electrode Quality
steps.instructions: Check electrodes for expiration date, adhesive integrity, and gel quality. Replace with fresh electrodes if necessary. Why: Poor skin contact can simulate lead faults.

CCR.Complaint: LIFEPAK 12 shows ‘Lead Off’ alarms intermittently with patient connected.
CCR.Cause: ECG lead cable pins slightly bent; patient electrodes poorly adhered.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced ECG lead cable and applied fresh electrodes. Verified error cleared and ECG waveform stable.

helpfulDetails: Indicator lights: ECG leads LED flashes red
Final device status: Operational, ready for patient use

title: Physio-Control LIFEPAK 12 - Battery Discharge / Poor Runtime
description: Troubleshooting guide for rapid battery discharge and reduced runtime in the LIFEPAK 12 MRx defibrillator.

steps.title: Check the Battery Charge Status
steps.instructions: Ensure the battery is seated correctly and locked into place. Verify the charge level via the device display. Reason: Improper seating can prevent full contact, causing false low-battery warnings.

steps.title: Inspect the Battery Physically
steps.instructions: Look for cracks, swelling, corrosion on contacts, or leaks. Reason: Physical damage can reduce capacity or create unsafe conditions.

steps.title: Test the Charger and Charging Cable
steps.instructions: Confirm the charger is plugged into a live outlet and indicator lights are normal. Swap the charger with a known-good unit if available. Reason: Faulty chargers or outlets are common causes of incomplete charging.

steps.title: Check for Accessory Draw
steps.instructions: Ensure no ECG/defibrillation leads, monitors, or other attachments are connected while charging. Reason: Connected accessories may draw power during charge, extending charging time and reducing effective runtime.

steps.title: Verify Environmental Conditions
steps.instructions: Charge and operate the device within the recommended temperature range (typically 15–30°C / 59–86°F). Reason: Extreme cold or heat can temporarily reduce battery performance.

steps.title: Perform a Device Self-Test
steps.instructions: Run a full self-test with the battery installed. Observe battery runtime estimate and any fault codes. Reason: Confirms whether the device recognizes the battery correctly and reports expected runtime.

steps.title: Swap with a Known-Good Battery
steps.instructions: Install a verified, fully charged battery and repeat self-test. Reason: Isolates whether the battery or the device electronics are the issue.

CCR.Complaint: Device battery drains too quickly; runtime less than 30 minutes on a full charge.
CCR.Cause: Battery seated correctly; charger, outlet, and environmental conditions verified; self-test confirms short runtime.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced with a verified battery; device sent for bench evaluation due to suspected internal battery degradation.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (removed from service if problem persists)

title: Physio-Control LIFEPAK 12 - Defibrillator Not Charging to Energy Level
description: Guide for troubleshooting LIFEPAK 12 devices that fail to reach selected defibrillation energy levels, focusing on external checks before internal repair.

steps.title: Verify Battery Condition
steps.instructions: Check that the battery is fully seated and properly locked in place. Confirm battery charge using the device’s battery indicator. Swap with a known good battery to rule out weak or defective battery. Reason: A weak battery may not supply enough current for full energy charge.

steps.title: Inspect Power Connections
steps.instructions: If on AC power, ensure the power cable is securely connected and outlet is live. Test outlet with a multimeter or another device. Reason: Insufficient power input can prevent full capacitor charge.

steps.title: Check Defibrillator Pads and Cables
steps.instructions: Ensure pads are correctly connected and not expired or damaged. Inspect ECG/defibrillation cables for visible breaks or wear. Reason: Open or shorted circuits may prevent safe charge completion.

steps.title: Attempt Charge with Minimal Configuration
steps.instructions: Remove patient cables and pads, leaving only the device and battery. Attempt to charge to the desired energy level. Reason: Isolates potential patient circuit issues.

steps.title: Observe Device Indicators and Error Messages
steps.instructions: Note any “Charge Failed,” “Low Energy,” or “Check Battery” messages. Record the behavior for work order documentation. Reason: Provides clues whether the issue is battery, capacitor, or internal electronics.

steps.title: Test with Another Device (if available)
steps.instructions: Attempt same charging procedure on a second LIFEPAK 12 using the same battery. Compare results to determine if the issue is with the battery or the device.

CCR.Complaint: Device will not charge to selected energy level; displays ‘Charge Failed’.
CCR.Cause: Battery tested good; cables and pads confirmed intact; issue persists with device only.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent to Physio-Control for repair.

helpfulDetails: Any indicator lights during attempted charge
Condition and expiration of pads and cables
Final device status: Out of Service

title: Physio-Control LIFEPAK 12 - Defibrillator Printer / Documentation Faults
description: Guide for troubleshooting printer and documentation issues on the LIFEPAK 12, focusing on external checks before internal repair.

steps.title: Verify Printer Power
steps.instructions: Ensure the printer module or integrated printer has power. Check any power switches or LEDs. Why: Without power, no printouts are possible.

steps.title: Check Paper Supply and Orientation
steps.instructions: Confirm there is paper in the printer tray or roll. Ensure it is installed correctly (not upside-down or jammed). Why: Improper paper loading is a common cause of print failures.

steps.title: Inspect Paper Path for Obstructions
steps.instructions: Remove any torn paper, debris, or foreign objects. Gently clean the paper feed path if needed. Why: Obstructions can prevent printing or cause misfeeds.

steps.title: Confirm Printer Connection / Module Seating
steps.instructions: Ensure the printer module is properly seated if removable. Check that all connectors and latches are secure. Why: Loose connections may prevent data transfer to the printer.

steps.title: Check Device Settings
steps.instructions: Verify print settings in the LIFEPAK 12 menu. Ensure event documentation printing is enabled. Why: Incorrect configuration may suppress printing.

steps.title: Test with a Known Good Paper Roll
steps.instructions: Swap the current paper with a fresh, compatible roll. Why: Paper type or quality issues can prevent clear printouts.

steps.title: Perform a Test Print / Self-Test
steps.instructions: Use the printer’s self-test function if available. Confirm if the printer prints independently of patient data. Why: Isolates printer hardware vs device software issues.

CCR.Complaint: Printer will not print ECG strips or event logs.
CCR.Cause: Paper loaded correctly, printer powered, connections verified, but print test failed.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent to Biomed for repair.

helpfulDetails: Error messages displayed on device screen

title: Physio-Control LIFEPAK 12 - Alarms Stuck or Muted
description: Guide for troubleshooting muted, stuck, or persistent alarms on the LIFEPAK 12 to ensure patient safety.

steps.title: Verify Alarm Volume Setting
steps.instructions: Check that the device’s alarm volume is set appropriately. Some devices have a dedicated volume knob or menu setting. Why: An accidental mute or low volume setting is the most common cause of inaudible alarms.

steps.title: Check the “Mute” or “Silence” Button
steps.instructions: Ensure the mute/silence button is not engaged. Press the button to cycle through audible alarm states. Why: Mute may have been activated by staff or accidentally held down.

steps.title: Inspect ECG / Lead Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure patient leads are securely connected. Some alarms remain “active” if leads are off or cables are damaged. Why: The device may continue generating alarm conditions due to poor signal detection.

steps.title: Verify Battery and Power Source
steps.instructions: Check the battery charge and AC power if available. Weak batteries can sometimes affect alarm operation. Why: Devices may disable or alter alarm behavior when power is low.

steps.title: Check for Software or Mode Settings
steps.instructions: Review current operating mode (e.g., monitor-only vs. defibrillator mode). Some modes reduce or suppress certain alarms. Why: Mode settings can change alarm priorities and thresholds.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn the device off, wait 10 seconds, and power back on. Observe if alarms reset and sound normally. Why: Temporary software or system glitches can cause stuck alarms.

CCR.Complaint: User reports that alarms are silent or persist without stopping.
CCR.Cause: Device alarm volume was muted and/or software glitch caused alarms to stick.
CCR.Resolution: Checked volume and mode settings, power cycled device; issue persisted. Device labeled Out of Service and sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Alarm volume and mute setting verified
Mode of operation (monitor, AED, or manual defibrillator)
Any observed alarm lights or persistent error indicators

title: Physio-Control LIFEPAK 15 - Touchscreen Unresponsiveness / Freezing
description: Guide for troubleshooting LIFEPAK 15 touchscreen that is unresponsive or freezes.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn the device off and remove the battery if accessible. Wait 30–60 seconds and reinsert the battery. Power the device back on. Why: A simple reboot can clear software glitches causing touchscreen unresponsiveness.

steps.title: Check Battery Charge Level
steps.instructions: Ensure the battery is fully charged and properly seated. Swap with a known-good battery if available. Why: Low voltage or poor battery contacts can cause intermittent touchscreen freezing.

steps.title: Inspect the Screen for Obstructions
steps.instructions: Clean the touchscreen with a soft, lint-free cloth. Remove any screen protectors or adhesives that may interfere. Why: Dirt, moisture, or debris can prevent accurate touch registration.

steps.title: Check for External Device Interference
steps.instructions: Remove USB drives, data cables, or other accessories temporarily. Power on and test touchscreen response. Why: Connected peripherals or damaged cables can affect touchscreen performance.

steps.title: Perform a Software Reset / Reboot
steps.instructions: If available, use the device’s system menu to perform a software reset. Ensure patient data is saved or exported before reset. Why: Freezing may be due to software hang, which a reset can resolve without hardware repair.

steps.title: Observe for Patterns
steps.instructions: Note if freezing occurs during specific functions (e.g., recording, alarm silencing, or menu navigation). Why: Patterns can help determine whether it’s software-related or touchscreen hardware degradation.

CCR.Complaint: Touchscreen unresponsive; cannot navigate menus or access patient data.
CCR.Cause: Observed touchscreen freezing regardless of battery swap or power cycle. No external obstructions or peripherals causing interference.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent to Physio-Control for bench evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Any connected peripherals at time of freezing
Final device status: Out of Service

title: Physio-Control LIFEPAK 15 - Battery Low / Failure to Hold Charge
description: Guide for troubleshooting LIFEPAK 15 batteries that fail to hold charge or show unexpected low battery warnings.

steps.title: Verify battery type and seating
steps.instructions: Ensure the battery is the correct LIFEPAK 15-approved battery model. Remove and reseat the battery to confirm proper contact with terminals. Improper seating can prevent charging or give false low battery readings.

steps.title: Check the battery charge status
steps.instructions: Connect the device to its standard AC charger and observe the charging LED indicators. Allow at least 2–3 hours for a full charge if the battery was fully depleted.

steps.title: Test with a known good battery
steps.instructions: Swap in a battery with a verified charge. If the device functions normally, the original battery is likely at fault.

steps.title: Inspect battery and charger connections
steps.instructions: Check for debris, corrosion, or bent terminals on both the battery and the device charger port. Clean gently with a dry, lint-free cloth if needed.

steps.title: Confirm charger functionality
steps.instructions: Test the AC charger with another LIFEPAK 15 unit if available. Verify that the outlet is live using a calibrated outlet tester.

steps.title: Check environmental conditions
steps.instructions: Batteries may fail to charge properly if too hot (>40°C / 104°F) or cold (<10°C / 50°F). Move device to a room-temperature environment and retry charging.

steps.title: Observe battery behavior during device operation
steps.instructions: Power on the device and note any rapid drop in battery percentage or low battery warnings. Confirm whether runtime is significantly below the manufacturer’s expected duration.

CCR.Complaint: Battery reads low shortly after charging; device powers off prematurely.
CCR.Cause: Battery failed to maintain charge despite proper seating and full charging cycle.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced battery with known good battery; removed original battery from service and tagged for evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Device ran for expected duration with replacement battery
Original battery labeled Out of Service

title: Physio-Control LIFEPAK 15 - ECG Misreading / Flatline Despite Leads Attached
description: Guide to troubleshoot ECG misreadings or flatlines on the LIFEPAK 15 by checking leads, cables, electrodes, settings, and external interference before escalation.

steps.title: Verify Lead Placement
steps.instructions: Ensure all ECG leads are connected to the correct patient locations. Check for lead reversal or loose connections; even one loose lead can cause a flatline or misreading. Proper placement ensures accurate detection of cardiac signals.

steps.title: Inspect ECG Cables and Electrodes
steps.instructions: Examine cables for visible damage, kinks, or fraying. Replace electrodes if they are dried out or past expiration. Confirm good skin contact and electrode gel integrity. Damaged cables or poor electrode adhesion can prevent signal transmission.

steps.title: Check Device Settings
steps.instructions: Verify the LIFEPAK 15 is in the correct monitoring mode (e.g., 3-lead vs. 12-lead). Confirm gain/amplitude settings and filter options are appropriate for patient condition. Incorrect settings may suppress or distort ECG waveform.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn the defibrillator off and back on. Reconnect the leads after power-up. Temporary software glitches can interfere with signal display.

steps.title: Test with Known Good Leads or Simulator
steps.instructions: Connect a known working set of leads or an ECG simulator if available. If waveform appears correctly, original leads or electrodes may be faulty. Confirms whether the issue is device or accessory-related.

steps.title: Check for External Interference
steps.instructions: Remove the patient and device from potential EMI sources (large motors, cell phones, other medical equipment). Electromagnetic interference can create false flatlines or noisy signals.

CCR.Complaint: User reports LIFEPAK 15 shows flatline ECG despite leads attached.
CCR.Cause: Leads, electrodes, or cable integrity were suspect; improper placement or dried-out electrodes possible.
CCR.Resolution: Checked lead connections and placement; replaced electrodes and cables. Verified correct device settings and power cycled. Device functioned normally after external adjustments. If unresolved, device labeled Out of Service and sent for repair.

title: Physio-Control LIFEPAK 15 - Failed Self-Test on Power-Up
description: Guide to troubleshoot LIFEPAK 15 devices failing automated self-tests during power-on.

steps.title: Verify battery status
steps.instructions: Ensure the battery is fully seated and charged. A low or poorly seated battery can cause self-test failure. Why: LIFEPAK 15 self-tests check battery health and connection first.

steps.title: Inspect battery and device contacts
steps.instructions: Remove battery and inspect terminals for corrosion, dirt, or debris. Clean contacts with a dry, lint-free cloth. Reinstall battery and ensure a snug fit. Why: Poor connection may trigger system errors during self-test.

steps.title: Check charger and power source
steps.instructions: Confirm the device is connected to a known working charger or AC outlet. Test the outlet with another device to ensure proper voltage. Why: Self-test may fail if battery charge is insufficient or unstable.

steps.title: Remove external accessories
steps.instructions: Disconnect ECG leads, paddles, and other attachments. Power cycle device without accessories. Why: Faulty or shorted accessories can cause self-test errors.

steps.title: Observe error codes/messages
steps.instructions: Take note of any messages displayed during the self-test (e.g., “Battery Low,” “Hardware Fault,” “Sensor Error”). This helps identify whether the issue is battery, electronics, or accessory related.

steps.title: Power cycle the device
steps.instructions: Turn off, wait 30 seconds, then power back on. Why: Temporary glitches can sometimes be cleared with a simple reset.

steps.title: Try a known-good battery
steps.instructions: If available, insert a fully charged, verified battery. Repeat power-on self-test. Why: Confirms whether the battery is the root cause.

CCR.Complaint: LIFEPAK 15 fails self-test on power-up, displaying error messages immediately.
CCR.Cause: Observed fully seated battery, clean contacts, and no external accessory faults. Device still fails self-test. Likely internal hardware/firmware issue.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent to authorized repair vendor for bench evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Any error codes or messages displayed
Final device status before sending for repair

title: Physio-Control LIFEPAK 15 - Charging Faults / Slow Charge
description: Guide for troubleshooting slow charging or charging errors on the LIFEPAK 15, emphasizing external checks and proper device handling.

steps.title: Verify Power Source
steps.instructions: Ensure the wall outlet or charging station is supplying correct voltage. Use a known working outlet if possible. Why: Inconsistent power can cause slow charging or failure.

steps.title: Check Charger Connection
steps.instructions: Inspect the charger cable for frays, cuts, or loose connections. Confirm the charger is properly seated on the LIFEPAK 15 battery or device. Why: Loose or damaged connections reduce current flow.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Condition
steps.instructions: Confirm battery is compatible and not past service life. Remove and reseat the battery to ensure proper contact. Why: Aging or improperly seated batteries charge slowly.

steps.title: Observe Device Indicators
steps.instructions: Check for error codes or LED status lights on the device and charger. Note any abnormal behavior during charging. Why: Indicators may help pinpoint whether the device or battery is at fault.

steps.title: Try a Known Good Battery
steps.instructions: Swap with a verified working battery if available. Why: Confirms whether the issue is battery-related or device-related.

steps.title: Test Alternate Charger (If Available)
steps.instructions: Use another compatible charger to rule out charger failure. Why: Chargers themselves can fail or underperform.

CCR.Complaint: User reported that the LIFEPAK 15 charges very slowly and sometimes displays a charging error.
CCR.Cause: Battery was seated loosely and charger connections were inconsistent; battery approaching end of service life.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated battery, confirmed charger functionality, replaced aging battery with tested unit. Device now charges within normal runtime.

title: Physio-Control LIFEPAK 20 / 20e - Printer or Event Recording Failure
description: Guide for troubleshooting printer or event recording failures on a LIFEPAK 20 / 20e defibrillator, emphasizing external checks before internal repair.

steps.title: Check Paper Supply
steps.instructions: Ensure the printer has paper installed correctly. Verify the paper type matches manufacturer specifications. A missing or incorrectly loaded paper roll is a common cause of printer failure.

steps.title: Inspect Printer Door and Latch
steps.instructions: Ensure the printer door is fully closed and latched. Many devices prevent printing if the printer is not properly seated.

steps.title: Check Printer Connections
steps.instructions: Verify the internal ribbon or cartridge (if present) is seated correctly. Inspect cables or connectors for damage or disconnection.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn the defibrillator off, wait 10–15 seconds, and turn it back on. Power cycling can reset the printer interface and clear minor software glitches.

steps.title: Verify Event Recording Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm that event recording is enabled in the device settings. Ensure proper ECG lead and pad connections, as missing input can prevent event capture.

steps.title: Test Print Function
steps.instructions: Use the device’s built-in self-test or 'print test' function. This isolates whether the issue is with the printer mechanism or with event recording itself.

steps.title: Check for Obstructions or Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect the printer path for debris, torn paper, or jammed mechanisms. Look for visible damage to the printer gears or rollers.

CCR.Complaint: Device did not print ECG after shock delivery; event record missing.
CCR.Cause: Printer paper was depleted and printer door not fully latched. Event recording enabled but no printout generated due to lack of paper and improper seating.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced printer paper, reseated printer door, ran print self-test successfully. Event recording verified functional.

title: Physio-Control LIFEPAK 20 / 20e - Shock Delivery Issues / Charging Errors
description: Guide for troubleshooting LIFEPAK 20 / 20e defibrillators that fail to charge or deliver shocks, prioritizing external checks and patient safety.

steps.title: Verify the device is powered on
steps.instructions:
Check that the main power switch is on and the display is functional.
A non-responsive unit can indicate dead batteries or a blown fuse.

steps.title: Check the battery status and connections
steps.instructions:
Ensure the battery is fully seated in its compartment.
Inspect for corrosion, debris, or physical damage on battery terminals.
Swap with a known good battery if available to rule out battery failure.

steps.title: Inspect charging system and cables
steps.instructions:
Check the AC power connection if the unit uses mains charging.
Confirm the charger indicator shows active charging.
Look for frayed, pinched, or damaged charging cables.

steps.title: Examine electrode pads and connections
steps.instructions:
Ensure pads are properly connected to the device and patient (or test load).
Verify the correct pad type for the model.
Inspect for torn or dried-out pads that can prevent proper shock delivery.

steps.title: Perform a device self-test
steps.instructions:
Navigate to the device’s built-in self-test function.
Note any error codes related to charging, shock delivery, or battery issues.
Document the exact error for work order purposes.

steps.title: Check environmental factors
steps.instructions:
Ensure the device is at proper operating temperature (not too cold or hot).
Remove any metal objects that could interfere with charge or shock delivery.

CCR.Complaint: Defibrillator fails to charge and will not deliver a shock.
CCR.Cause: Battery seated properly but unit fails self-test; charging circuit and cables verified as functional.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for authorized repair.

helpfulDetails: Battery voltage and status at the time of testing
Electrode pad type and expiration date
Final device status (OOS and sent for repair)

title: Physio-Control LIFEPAK 20 / 20e - ECG Display Errors or Artifacts
description: Guide to troubleshoot ECG display errors or artifacts on a LIFEPAK 20 / 20e defibrillator, focusing on external causes before internal failure.

steps.title: Check Patient Connection
steps.instructions: Verify electrodes are correctly placed and fully adhered to the patient’s skin. Ensure proper skin preparation (clean, dry, and free from oils or gels). Why: Poor contact or loose electrodes are the most common cause of artifacts.

steps.title: Inspect ECG Cables
steps.instructions: Check for visible damage, kinks, or breaks in the cables. Ensure connectors are fully seated in the device. Swap with a known-good ECG cable if available. Why: Damaged or loose cables can introduce intermittent or noisy signals.

steps.title: Check Electrode Quality and Expiration
steps.instructions: Confirm electrodes are within their expiration date and compatible with LIFEPAK 20 / 20e. Replace old or dried-out electrodes. Why: Electrodes lose conductivity over time, causing artifacts.

steps.title: Verify Device Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm proper ECG lead configuration is selected (e.g., 3-lead vs. 5-lead). Ensure filters (if available) are correctly set for the patient and monitoring needs. Why: Incorrect configuration can produce abnormal display or baseline wander.

steps.title: Assess Environmental Interference
steps.instructions: Remove sources of electromagnetic interference (EMI) such as mobile phones, infusion pumps, or nearby electrical equipment. Check that the patient is not shivering or moving excessively. Why: EMI or motion artifacts can distort the ECG waveform.

steps.title: Perform Device Self-Test
steps.instructions: Run the built-in ECG test or device self-test function. Why: Confirms whether the device circuitry and display are functioning correctly.

CCR.Complaint: ECG waveform is showing baseline wander and intermittent noise.
CCR.Cause: Electrodes were partially detached, and the ECG cable showed minor wear. Environmental EMI from nearby infusion pumps was also present.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced electrodes, secured ECG cable connections, minimized nearby EMI. Device self-test passed after corrections.

helpfulDetails: Electrode placement and skin prep confirmed

title: Physio-Control LIFEPAK 20 / 20e - Pad / Electrode Detection Errors
description: Guide for Clinical Engineering to troubleshoot pad or electrode detection errors on a LIFEPAK 20 / 20e defibrillator, focusing on external checks before internal failure.

steps.title: Verify Pad Type and Expiration
steps.instructions:
Confirm that the pads are approved for LIFEPAK 20 / 20e.
Check expiration dates; expired pads may fail detection.
Why: Incorrect or expired pads can prevent the device from recognizing electrodes.

steps.title: Inspect Pads and Connectors
steps.instructions:
Look for tears, cracks, dried gel, or corrosion on connectors.
Ensure that pad connectors are clean and free of debris.
Why: Damaged or dirty connections can interrupt the signal to the device.

steps.title: Reconnect Pads Firmly
steps.instructions:
Detach and reattach pads to the device, ensuring a full click into the connector.
Why: Loose connections are a common cause of detection errors.

steps.title: Check Cable Integrity
steps.instructions:
Inspect electrode cables for cuts, kinks, or visible damage.
Swap with a known-good cable if available.
Why: Damaged cables can prevent proper detection.

steps.title: Confirm Device Settings
steps.instructions:
Verify the device is set to recognize the correct pad type if adjustable.
Why: Some LIFEPAK devices allow configuration for adult vs. pediatric pads.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions:
Turn off the device, wait 10 seconds, then turn back on.
Why: Sometimes the internal detection system resets after a power cycle.

steps.title: Test on Known-Good Pads
steps.instructions:
Attach a new set of LIFEPAK-compatible pads to confirm if the device recognizes them.
Why: Confirms whether the issue is with the device or the pads.

CCR.Complaint: pads not detected when attempting defibrillation.
CCR.Cause: Observed pads were expired; connectors had dried gel and minor corrosion.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced pads with new LIFEPAK-approved set; confirmed device recognized electrodes and passed self-test.

helpfulDetails: Any unusual sounds or device behavior

title: Physio-Control LIFEPAK 20 / 20e - Sensor or Accessory Connection Errors
description: Troubleshoot sensor or accessory connection errors on a LIFEPAK 20 / 20e defibrillator focusing on external checks before internal failure.

steps.title: Visual Inspection of Cables and Connectors
steps.instructions: Check all lead wires, ECG cables, paddles, and other accessory connections for visible damage, frayed wires, bent pins, or corrosion. Ensure connectors are clean and fully seated. Why: Loose or damaged connections are the most common cause of accessory errors.

steps.title: Verify Accessory Compatibility
steps.instructions: Confirm the electrodes, paddles, and any other accessories are compatible with LIFEPAK 20 / 20e. Check manufacturer labeling or part numbers. Why: Incompatible accessories can prevent proper communication and trigger alarms.

steps.title: Inspect Battery and Power Source
steps.instructions: Ensure the defibrillator battery is properly seated and charged. Verify the device operates on AC power if applicable. Why: Low or intermittent power can cause accessory detection errors.

steps.title: Reconnect Accessories
steps.instructions: Disconnect and reconnect all leads, electrodes, and paddles one at a time. Observe for any error messages after each reconnection. Why: Re-seating connections can clear intermittent communication faults.

steps.title: Check Device Self-Test / Calibration
steps.instructions: Run the built-in self-test according to the device prompts. Note any errors related to sensors or accessories. Why: Confirms whether the device can communicate with connected accessories and helps isolate the fault.

steps.title: Swap Known-Good Accessories
steps.instructions: Replace suspect leads, paddles, or electrodes with known working equivalents. Re-test connection and operation. Why: Confirms whether the issue is with the device or the accessory.

CCR.Complaint: Defibrillator unable to detect ECG leads and paddles; accessory disconnected alarm displayed.
CCR.Cause: ECG cables had loose connectors; paddles seated improperly; battery partially seated.
CCR.Resolution: Re-seated all leads and paddles, verified proper battery connection, ran self-test successfully, no further alarms.

helpfulDetails: Serial numbers of suspect leads or paddles
